title: 10 best herbal decoctions for alzheimer’s disease
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for Alzheimer's disease are concentrated plant-based extracts made by steeping herbs in hot water, which have been traditionally used to improve cognitive function and memory. These decoctions help by increasing blood flow to the brain, reducing inflammation, and improving communication between brain cells. Examples of herbal decoctions that show promise include Ginkgo biloba, Bacopa monnieri, and Ashwagandha. By using these decoctions, individuals with Alzheimer's disease may experience improved memory recall, enhanced mental clarity, and a reduced risk of further cognitive decline, allowing them to maintain their independence and live more fulfilling lives.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and terpenes that have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory. The decoctions' ability to cross the blood-brain barrier allows for direct targeting of the affected areas in the brain, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to neurodegeneration. Additionally, the decoctions' ability to promote the growth of new neurons and neural connections may help to compensate for damaged areas, supporting overall brain health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Maidenhair tree decoctions contain antioxidants that help protect brain cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective: The decoction has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, helping to prevent or slow down the death of brain cells and promoting neuronal survival, which is critical in managing Alzheimer's disease.

Cholinergic: Maidenhair tree decoctions contain cholinergic compounds that can help improve cognitive function and memory by enhancing the activity of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in learning and memory processes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help prevent oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are known to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has neuroprotective properties, which may help reduce neuronal damage and death associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Quercetin: A type of flavonoid, quercetin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce the progression of Alzheimer's disease by inhibiting the formation of amyloid plaques in the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve memory and cognitive function.
Seeds: The seeds are also used due to their high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Barks: The barks are used due to their potential to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the brain, which may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers from mature trees in autumn.
Crush the ginkgo biloba material into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes then remove from heat source.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because it contains bioactive compounds that have been shown to enhance cognitive function, improve memory retention, and promote neuroprotection. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in brahmi decoctions help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are common hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, brahmi is believed to increase the production of neurotransmitters such as acetylcholine, which plays a crucial role in memory formation and retrieval.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Brahmi decoction contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage brain cells and contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Cognitive-enhancing properties: The active compounds in Brahmi, such as brahmine and amalaki, have been shown to improve memory, attention, and cognitive function, making it an effective natural treatment for Alzheimer's disease.
Neuroprotective properties: Brahmi decoction has been found to have neuroprotective effects, which can help protect against the death of brain cells and slow down the progression of the disease.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of glycosides that have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function by enhancing the expression of neurotrophic factors and promoting the growth of new neurons.

Bacopa saponins: Bacopa saponins have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Monnierin: Monnierin is a triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to have neuroprotective effects by inhibiting the acetylcholinesterase enzyme, which breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in memory and cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of bacosides, which are the primary bioactive compounds responsible for the medicinal properties of Bacopa monnieri.
Stems: Stems are also used, as they contain bacosides and other compounds that contribute to the plant's neuroprotective and cognitive-enhancing effects.
Roots: Roots are sometimes used, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, as they are believed to possess similar bioactive compounds to the leaves and stems, although in smaller quantities.
remedy_recipe: Gather 5 grams of dried bacopa monnieri roots and 500 ml of water for decoction.
Chop the dried roots into smaller pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because it has been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are hallmarks of this devastating neurodegenerative disorder. The decoction is believed to promote healthy neuronal function, improve memory and cognitive functions, and even slow down the progression of the disease by protecting against beta-amyloid plaque formation, a key characteristic of Alzheimer's.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Asiatic Pennywort decoctions have high antioxidant capacity, which helps to protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, contributing to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Cholinergic activity: The decoctions contain compounds that stimulate cholinergic receptors, improving cognitive function, memory, and attention, which are often impaired in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Asiatic Pennywort decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the brain, a key factor in Alzheimer's pathology, and thereby slow down disease progression.
remedy_constituents: Asiaticoside: Asiaticoside has neuroprotective properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are contributing factors to the development and progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Madecassoside: Madecassoside has been shown to promote the growth of new neurons and improve cognitive function by enhancing the expression of neurotrophic factors, which support the survival and growth of neurons.

Asiatic acid: Asiatic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect neurons from damage and death, and may also improve memory and cognitive function by enhancing the activity of neurotransmitters such as acetylcholine.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Roots: The roots of Centella asiatica contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to possess neuroprotective properties, potentially beneficial in treating Alzheimer's disease.
Stems: The stems of Centella asiatica are also used in decoctions due to their content of bioactive compounds that may help in improving cognitive function and memory.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried centella asiatica leaves and 1l of water for decoction.
Weigh and combine the dried centella asiatica leaves in a heat-resistant glass container.
Bring the water to a boil in a separate pot then carefully pour it over the leaves.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins that have been shown to possess potent neuroprotective properties. These compounds may help reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and amyloid-β protein deposition in the brain, all of which are associated with the progression of Alzheimer's disease. By targeting these underlying mechanisms, skullcap decoctions may help slow down cognitive decline and improve memory function in individuals affected by this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Herbal skullcap decoctions have antioxidant properties that help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are thought to contribute to the development and progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Skullcap contains compounds that inhibit the enzyme acetylcholinesterase, which breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter important for memory and cognitive function. By inhibiting this enzyme, skullcap may help increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, which could improve cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's.

Inflammation reduction: Skullcap decoctions have been shown to reduce inflammation in the brain, which is thought to be a key contributor to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. By reducing inflammation, skullcap may help slow or halt the progression of the disease.
remedy_constituents: Baicalein: Baicalein, a flavonoid flavone, has neuroprotective effects that help prevent neuroinflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are key factors in the development of Alzheimer's disease.

Baicalin: Baicalin, a flavonoid glycoside, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce neuroinflammation and oxidative stress, which are crucial in slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Wogonin: Wogonin, a flavone, has neuroprotective effects that help inhibit the formation of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and also has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that contribute to its neuroprotective effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain baicalein and baicalin, which have neuroprotective properties that help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, particularly baicalein and baicalin, which have been shown to improve cognitive function and reduce neuroinflammation in Alzheimer's disease patients.

Roots (again, as it is the most used part): The root extract of Scutellaria baicalensis has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to improve memory and cognitive function, which is beneficial in treating Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 grams of dried scutellaria baicalensis root and place it in a heat-resistant glass container.
Pour 250 milliliters of boiling water over the scutellaria baicalensis root and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the root fragments.
Discard the solids and transfer the liquid to a clean glass container for storage.
Refrigerate the decoction at a temperature of 4 degrees celsius or below for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its unique ability to calm the mind and reduce anxiety, which are common symptoms experienced by individuals with the condition. The aromatic compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, both of which are crucial for cognitive function and memory consolidation. Additionally, lavender has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to neurodegeneration and cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's.
Neuroprotective properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in lavender have been shown to inhibit the formation of amyloid plaques and beta-amyloid peptides, key hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease.

Anxiolytic and anti-stress properties: Lavender's calming effects may help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common comorbidities with Alzheimer's, reducing symptoms such as agitation, aggression, and sleep disturbances.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has neuroprotective properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, linalyl acetate has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce the severity of Alzheimer's disease symptoms by protecting brain cells from damage.

Apigenin: This flavonoid has been found to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to prevent the formation of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and reduce oxidative stress in the brain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most used part due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to their use in decoctions for Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or 10-15 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia leaves and flowers for decoction.
Chop the leaves and flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped lavandula angustifolia with 1 liter of water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes or 30-40 minutes for a stronger decoction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, have been shown to stimulate cognitive function and improve memory by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. Additionally, maypop has been found to inhibit the production of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, thereby slowing down its progression and alleviating symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maypop decoctions have antioxidant properties that help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Cholinesterase inhibition: Maypop contains compounds that inhibit the activity of cholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter important for memory and cognitive function. By inhibiting cholinesterase, maypop may help to increase levels of acetylcholine in the brain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation in the brain, which is believed to play a role in the development and progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Harpagide: This iridoid glycoside has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, potentially helping to slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

Isovitexin: This flavone glycoside has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against neurodegeneration and improve cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

Ferulic acid: This phenolic acid has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which may help to improve cognitive function and memory.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, which may help to alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Fruits: They are used due to their high antioxidant content, which may help to protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and 1 quart of water for this herbal decoction.
Combine the dried flowers with water in a saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort decoctions help with Alzheimer's disease because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have neuroprotective properties. This property helps in protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the development of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, St John's Wort decoctions may also improve cognitive function by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and acetylcholine, which play a crucial role in memory formation and retrieval.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to inhibit the breakdown of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in memory formation and retrieval, which can help slow down cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's.

Hypotensive properties: St John's Wort decoctions may help reduce blood pressure, which is often elevated in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, and high blood pressure has been linked to an increased risk of cognitive decline.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds present in St John's Wort decoctions can help protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has shown neuroprotective properties and can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are contributing factors to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect neurons from damage and may improve cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

Amentoflavone: Amentoflavone, a flavonoid dimer, has been shown to have neuroprotective effects and can inhibit the formation of amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, thereby slowing its progression.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in hyperforin, a compound that may have neuroprotective effects and help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect against neurodegeneration.
Seeds: They have a higher concentration of hypericin, a compound that may have neuroprotective and antioxidant effects, potentially contributing to the treatment of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the plant hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Clean the plant material by gently brushing off dirt and debris from the leaves.
Measure 2-3 tablespoons of the dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves for every 1 cup of water.
Combine the measured plant material with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes then strain the liquid before consuming the decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to cross the blood-brain barrier and protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. These conditions are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease by damaging brain cells and leading to the accumulation of beta-amyloid plaques. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, silymarin may help slow down or even stop the damage caused by these factors, potentially improving cognitive function and memory in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: This polyphenolic compound has been shown to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to neurodegeneration in Alzheimer's disease.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in milk thistle decoctions may help improve memory and cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and protecting against beta-amyloid plaque formation.
Lactone: Milk thistle lactone has been found to have neuroprotective effects, including reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in neurons, which can help slow down disease progression.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin, a mixture of flavonoids and polyphenols, helps with Alzheimer's disease by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that protect against neurodegeneration and cognitive decline.

Flavonoids (specifically isosilic acid and silidianin): These flavonoids help with Alzheimer's disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of the disease.

Polyphenolic acids (specifically caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These polyphenolic acids help with Alzheimer's disease by inhibiting the formation of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of the disease, and also by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Silybum marianum are most commonly used to make decoctions for Alzheimer's disease due to their high content of silymarin, a flavonoid complex with neuroprotective properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Silybum marianum are also used to make decoctions for Alzheimer's disease, as they contain silymarin and other flavonoids that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

Fruits: The fruits of Silybum marianum are sometimes used to make decoctions for Alzheimer's disease, as they are rich in silymarin and other bioactive compounds that may help protect against neurodegenerative diseases.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50g of silybum marianum flowers and leaves from a clean environment to prevent contamination.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a warm place for 2-3 hours to remove excess moisture.
Weigh out 10-20g of dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves per 250ml of water for decoction.
Boil 250ml of water in a pot and add the weighed out silybum marianum flowers and leaves for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and neuroinflammation in the brain. Curcumin, a bioactive compound found in turmeric, has been demonstrated to inhibit the aggregation of beta-amyloid peptides and tau proteins, two hallmark features of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, turmeric decoctions may also improve cognitive function by increasing blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, ultimately reducing symptoms of memory loss and improving overall quality of life for individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Curcumin's ability to inhibit Amyloid-β aggregation: Turmeric decoctions contain curcumin, which has been shown to inhibit the aggregation of Amyloid-β peptides, a key hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, thereby reducing the formation of neurotoxic plaques in the brain.

Modulation of tau protein phosphorylation: Curcuminoids have been found to modulate the phosphorylation of tau protein, which is involved in the development of neurofibrillary tangles (NFTs) - a characteristic feature of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC has been shown to have neuroprotective effects by inhibiting the production of amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and reducing neuroinflammation.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the progression of Alzheimer's disease by inhibiting the production of inflammatory cytokines and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high content of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help prevent or slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa contain curcumin, which has been shown to have neuroprotective effects and may help improve memory and cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

Seeds: The seeds of Curcuma longa contain compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce the risk of developing Alzheimer's disease or slow down its progression.
remedy_recipe: Wash 1 teaspoon of dried curcuma longa root in cold water to remove impurities.
Boil 2 cups of water in a saucepan over high heat for 5 minutes.
Add the washed curcuma longa root to the boiling water and reduce the heat to medium.
Simmer the mixture for 10 minutes or until the liquid reduces to 1 cup.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its unique composition, which includes cinnamaldehyde, a potent compound that has been shown to inhibit the accumulation of amyloid-beta peptides in the brain. These peptides are a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease and are thought to contribute to its progression. By reducing their levels, Ceylon cinnamon decoctions may help slow down or even halt the progression of the disease, thereby improving cognitive function and quality of life for individuals affected by it.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ceylon cinnamon decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to neurodegeneration and memory loss associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Inhibition of amyloid-β formation: The decoction's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to inhibit the production of amyloid-β peptides, a hallmark protein accumulation in the brain that is characteristic of Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective effects: Ceylon cinnamon decoctions have been found to exhibit neuroprotective properties by reducing the release of glutamate, a neurotransmitter that can be toxic to neurons at high levels, and preventing the activation of pro-apoptotic proteins that contribute to cell death in Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce neuroinflammation and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Eugenol: This phenolic compound has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, potentially by inhibiting the formation of amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.

Lignans: These polyphenolic compounds may help improve cognitive function and memory by increasing the activity of enzymes involved in the breakdown of amyloid-beta peptides, which are implicated in the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have shown potential in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Barks: The bark contains cinnamaldehyde, which has been studied for its neuroprotective properties and potential to improve cognitive function.
Stems: The stems of Cinnamomum verum contain a compound called linalool, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects that may help alleviate Alzheimer's symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark, preferably from sri lanka, for decoction.
Boil 250ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a rolling boil for 5 minutes.
Add the cinnamomum verum bark to the boiling water and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess neuroprotective properties. The decoctions' antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the development of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, the decoctions' ability to stimulate blood flow and oxygenation to the brain may also play a role in improving cognitive function and memory recall, making them a potential adjunctive therapy for managing symptoms of this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ilex latifolia has antioxidant compounds that help protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Inflammation reduction: The plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease and can contribute to cognitive decline.

Neuroprotective properties: Ilex latifolia has been shown to have neuroprotective effects by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as acetylcholine, which helps to improve memory and cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, dog holly roots have been traditionally used to treat cognitive impairment and memory loss.
Barks: The barks of the Dog Holly plant contain flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for neuroprotection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ruscus aculeatus roots and dry them for future use.
Combine 30 grams of dried ruscus aculeatus root with 1000 milliliters of cold water.
Steep the mixture in a heat-resistant container at 70 degrees celsius for 45 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to inhibit the formation of beta-amyloid plaques in the brain, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, licorice root has been found to enhance cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and reducing oxidative stress. This natural remedy has the potential to slow down the progression of the disease and alleviate its symptoms, providing hope for those affected by this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially slowing down neurodegeneration associated with Alzheimer's.
Isomaltose: May inhibit the production of beta-amyloid peptides, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, by suppressing the activity of enzymes involved in their formation.
Licochalcone A: Has been shown to exhibit anti-aggregatory effects on beta-amyloid peptides, which may help prevent the accumulation of toxic beta-amyloid plaques in the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's.

Rhyzomes: Containing licoricidin and isoliquiritigenin, compounds that have been shown to have neuroprotective effects by inhibiting beta-amyloid protein aggregation, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Measure 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a clean glass container for preparation.
Boil 500 milliliters of distilled water in a pot and pour it over the measured root in a heat-resistant bowl.
Simmer the mixture for 20 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its unique ability to soothe and protect the brain. The mucilages present in marshmallows have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common hallmarks of the disease. Additionally, marshmallow's antioxidant properties help to clear amyloid plaques, a key characteristic of Alzheimer's, from the brain tissue. By doing so, marshmallow decoctions may help to slow down or even halt the progression of the disease, providing hope for individuals affected by this devastating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow decoction has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is believed to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow contains antioxidants that can neutralize free radicals and protect neurons from oxidative stress, which can damage brain cells and contribute to cognitive decline.

Glycogenic activity: Marshmallow decoction has been shown to stimulate glycogen synthesis in the brain, which may help improve memory and cognitive function by providing an alternative source of energy for neurons.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the brain against inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like triterpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit beta-amyloid protein aggregation, a hallmark of Alzheimer's.
remedy_recipe: Gently boil one tablespoon of dried marshmallow root in two cups of distilled water for ten minutes.
Steep the mixture for an additional twenty minutes to allow for maximum extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Combine the resulting liquid with one cup of cooled green tea and stir well to create a balanced blend.
Refrigerate the final mixture at 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to three days before consumption as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because its flavonoids and apigenin have been shown to improve cognitive function by reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and beta-amyloid plaque accumulation in the brain. Additionally, chamomile's GABA-mimetic effects can calm anxious minds and promote relaxation, which is essential for individuals with Alzheimer's who often experience agitation and anxiety. By soothing the nervous system, chamomile decoctions may also help reduce stress, a known exacerbating factor of the disease.
remedy_properties: Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid compound found in chamomile, has been shown to inhibit the production of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and reduce inflammation in the brain.

Chamazulene: Chamazulene, a sesquiterpene lactone present in chamomile, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect against oxidative stress and neurodegeneration in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Alpha-bisabolol: Alpha-bisabolol, a monoterpene found in chamomile, has been shown to have neuroprotective effects by reducing glutamate toxicity, a common feature of neurodegenerative disorders like Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to have neuroprotective effects and may help to reduce symptoms of Alzheimer's.
remedy_recipe: Measure carefully 2 tablespoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and place them in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Boil 4 cups of filtered water in a kettle or on the stovetop and pour it over the chamomile flowers in the cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength of flavor and medicinal properties.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because they have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory retention. The root of the valerian plant contains compounds that have been found to inhibit acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter essential for memory formation and retrieval. By increasing levels of acetylcholine in the brain, valerian decoctions may help to slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease and improve symptoms such as confusion and disorientation. Additionally, valerian has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Valerian decoctions have been shown to exhibit antioxidant activity, which helps protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, contributing to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective effects: Valerian contains compounds that have neuroprotective properties, which may help prevent or slow down neuronal damage and degeneration associated with Alzheimer's disease, such as amyloid-β peptide accumulation.

GABA-enhancing activity: Valerian has been found to enhance the activity of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep, anxiety, and stress, which are common symptoms of Alzheimer's disease; GABA deficiency has also been linked to cognitive decline.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, also known as the underground stem, contains a higher concentration of valerenic acid, which has been found to have neuroprotective effects and may help reduce oxidative stress in the brain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried valeriana officinalis root and 2l of water.
Combine the dried root and water in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Boil the mixture for 30 minutes then reduce heat and simmer for an additional hour.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to inhibit beta-amyloid plaque formation in the brain. This buildup of plaques is a hallmark characteristic of Alzheimer's disease and is thought to contribute to cognitive decline. By reducing this plaque accumulation, lemon balm decoctions may help slow down or even halt the progression of the disease, thereby improving memory and cognitive function. Additionally, lemon balm's calming properties can help alleviate anxiety and stress associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Lemon balm extracts have been shown to inhibit acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that plays a key role in memory formation and retrieval. By inhibiting this enzyme, lemon balm may help increase the levels of acetylcholine in the brain.
GABA-mimetic properties: Lemon balm decoctions have been found to bind to GABA receptors in the brain, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, roots are used to create a decoction that can help alleviate cognitive decline and improve memory function.
Seeds: Containing high levels of fatty acids, seeds are used to create a soothing decoction that can calm inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis flowers and pour into a heat-resistant glass cup.
Boil one cup of pure water in a kettle or on the stovetop and carefully pour over the dried flowers.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, covered with a lid, to release the bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another heat-resistant cup, pressing on the solids to extract all liquids.
Drink the melissa officinalis decoction immediately, while still warm, in three divided doses throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a rich source of antioxidants, specifically apigenin and luteolin, which have been shown to have neuroprotective properties. These compounds have been found to reduce inflammation in the brain, oxidative stress, and amyloid-beta peptide accumulation, all of which are key contributors to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. By targeting these underlying mechanisms, pot marigold decoctions may help slow down or even halt the cognitive decline associated with this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold decoctions have high antioxidant activity, which helps to neutralize free radicals in the brain and reduce oxidative stress, a key contributor to Alzheimer's disease.
Inflammation reduction: The decoction has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the brain that is associated with neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to inhibit acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in memory and cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to have neuroprotective properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and antioxidants, which may help improve cognitive function and memory by reducing inflammation and oxidative damage in the brain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh calendula officinalis flowers with 250 milliliters of boiling distilled water.
Reduce heat to low simmer and allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating it in an airtight container at 4°c for up to three days or freezing for later use.
Take one to two teaspoons of the cooled decoction orally twice daily, 30 minutes after breakfast and dinner, with meals that contain healthy fats.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its ability to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain. The decoction contains compounds like parthenolide, which has been shown to inhibit the formation of amyloid plaques, a hallmark of the disease. Additionally, the decoction's antioxidant properties help protect neurons from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy cognitive function and memory. This natural approach may complement traditional treatments for Alzheimer's disease, offering hope for improving symptoms and slowing disease progression.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Kansas coneflower decoctions have been shown to reduce inflammation in the brain, which is a known contributor to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
Antioxidant properties: The decoction contains antioxidants that can help protect brain cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially slowing down cognitive decline.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Kansas coneflower has been found to inhibit acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down the neurotransmitter acetylcholine, which is important for memory and cognitive function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to inhibit acetylcholinesterase activity, potentially improving cognitive function and memory.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried echinacea angustifolia roots in sufficient quantity for your needs.
Measure out two teaspoons of the dried root material per eight ounces of water needed.
Combine the measured echinacea angustifolia root with filtered or spring water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce heat to simmer for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because it contains compounds that have been shown to enhance cognitive function and reduce inflammation in the brain. The diuretic properties of dandelion can help remove toxins and heavy metals from the body, which may contribute to the progression of the disease. Additionally, the antioxidants present in dandelion have been found to protect against oxidative stress, a key factor in Alzheimer's pathology. By improving blood flow to the brain and reducing inflammation, dandelion decoctions may help slow down or even halt the progression of this debilitating disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Dandelion contains antioxidants like vitamins A, C, and E, which can help neutralize free radicals in the brain, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The bioactive compounds in dandelion, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the brain, which may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease like memory loss and cognitive decline.

Neuroprotective properties: Dandelion extracts contain compounds that have been found to promote neuroprotection by increasing the production of neurotrophic factors, such as BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor), which is essential for maintaining healthy brain function and preventing neuronal degeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant are used in medicinal decoctions because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective against Alzheimer's disease symptoms.

Fruits: Dandelion fruits (seeds) are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried taraxacum officinale herb for every eight ounces of water.
Heat the water in a saucepan over medium heat for ten minutes or until it reaches boiling point.
Add the dried taraxacum officinale herb to the boiling water and reduce the heat to low simmer.
Allow the decoction to steep for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half its original volume.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container for immediate use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to slow down the progression of the disease. The herb's active compounds, such as carnosic acid and rosmanol, have been found to improve memory and cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the hippocampus, a region critical for learning and memory. By incorporating rosemary decoctions into their daily routine, individuals with Alzheimer's may experience improved mental clarity and reduced symptoms of the disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Rosemary decoctions have antioxidant properties that help protect the brain from oxidative stress, which is thought to contribute to the development and progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in rosemary decoctions may help reduce inflammation in the brain, which is a common feature of Alzheimer's disease and can exacerbate its symptoms.

Cognitive-enhancing flavonoids: Rosemary contains flavonoids, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory by increasing blood flow to the brain and protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves have been traditionally used to improve memory and cognitive function, which is thought to be beneficial in managing Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately 10 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers for each serving.
Combine the measured herb with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a stainless steel pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes to extract beneficial compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 24 hours before use.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to Alzheimer's disease that are treated with herbal decoctions are vascular dementia, Lewy body dementia, and age-related cognitive decline. Herbal decoctions like Ginkgo biloba, Bacopa monnieri, and Ashwagandha have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to improve memory, cognitive function, and mental clarity. These herbs may also help reduce symptoms of depression, anxiety, and stress associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: I can't help with that request.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Metabolic brain disease, the researchers conducted a systematic search using Ovid MEDLINE, CINAHL, Cochrane, and PubMed databases and reference lists up to November 30, 2021, to explore the role of natural alternatives in the treatment of Alzheimer's disease. The study found that certain herbs, such as Gingko Biloba, Melissa Officinalis, Salvia officinalis, Ginseng, and saffron, either alone or in combination with curcumin, low-fat diet, NuAD-Trail, and soy lecithin, showed significant positive effects on Alzheimer's disease. These herbal decoctions may be beneficial in treating Alzheimer's disease, particularly when used in combination with FDA-approved drugs, which have been found to have far better effects than allopathic treatment alone.
